channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Así es como Lauren Sánchez Bezos es feliz
item.description: Como la mitad de una pareja mega poderosa, Sánchez Bezos parece haber influido en los superricos para que dejen de disculparse y empiecen a disfrutar.
item.dc:creator: Amy Chozick

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Pfluger para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lauren Sánchez Bezos trata la búsqueda y difusión de la alegría como una especie de mandato.

item.title: Pete Hegseth y su evangelio de destrucción
item.description: El secretario de Defensa cree que está comandando el ejército de Dios.
item.dc:creator: Frank Bruni
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Su devoción por las canciones de amor lo llevó a la cárcel, pero no lo silenció
item.description: La prisión no pudo conseguir que Nguyen Van Loc, perseguido por interpretar la vieja música romántica de Hanói durante la guerra de Vietnam, dejara de cantar.
item.dc:creator: Damien Cave and Linh Pham

media:content.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times

item.title: Picture Prompt: ‘Euphoria’ Returns
item.description: Are you a fan? Did you watch the season 3 premiere?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: 80s Tomorrow, 50s Next Week? New York Rides a Weather Roller Coaster.
item.description: Although Monday wasn’t as springlike as expected, people still were drawn to the beach. By midweek, the city is likely to see summer temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Emily Lang

media:content.media:credit: Brittainy Newman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A couple bundled up to sit on the boardwalk at Brighton Beach, Brooklyn.

item.title: Here’s What to Know About Weinstein’s Third Rape Trial in New York
item.description: Harvey Weinstein is scheduled to go on trial for a third time in six years on the same charge. An appeals court overturned his first conviction, and the second trial ended in a hung jury.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko

media:content.media:description: Harvey Weinstein, 74, will be tried for the third time in Manhattan.

item.title: Trump and New York Are in a Tug of War Over Medicaid Coverage
item.description: With tough new regulations looming in the president’s domestic policy bill, officials are coming up with plans to keep New Yorkers insured.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein
item.category: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services

media:content.media:description: For Dr. Alister Martin, New York City’s new health commissioner, keeping New Yorkers insured could prove to be the main challenge of his tenure.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 14, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: ‘It’s on the App’: A Police Chief’s $4.5 Million Gambling Secret
item.description: New Haven’s police chief, Karl Jacobson, resigned abruptly after his deputies saw red flags, including missing money. He has pleaded not guilty to embezzling city money to gamble on sports.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko

media:content.media:credit: City of New Haven, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Karl Jacobson served as New Haven’s police chief for three years before his career unraveled.

item.title: Is the Jewish Defense League Making a Comeback?
item.description: The Jewish Defense League had long been considered inactive. But an arrest in a plot to kill a Palestinian activist shed light on an apparent resurgence of far-right Zionism.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Yisrael Yaacob Ben Avraham, the founder of J.D.L. 613, is an admirer of Meir Kahane, who created the original Jewish Defense League.

item.title: Geneva Watch Week Seeks to Brighten Watchmaking’s Future
item.description: At Watches and Wonders, dozens of brands will be introducing new collections.
item.dc:creator: Robin Swithinbank

item.title: The TAG Heuer Monaco Evergraph Sparks a Revolution
item.description: The timepiece updates the square-cased watch design from the 1960s with an inventive chronograph movement.
item.dc:creator: Robin Swithinbank

item.title: Late Night Takes Sides in the President vs. the Pope
item.description: Jimmy Kimmel said President Trump’s social media post aimed at Pope Leo XIV “is what happens when you sell Bibles instead of reading them.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Word of the Day: hierarchy
item.description: This word has appeared in 1,333 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ¿El tema del momento en la manosfera? El servicio militar de Barron Trump
item.description: Casi tan pronto como comenzaron los bombardeos a Irán a finales de febrero, empezaron a circular memes e imágenes generadas por IA que insinuaban que el hijo de Donald Trump debería ir a la guerra.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Lo que las alergias estacionales le hacen a tu cuerpo
item.description: Un desglose de por qué tu sistema inmunitario trata al polen como a un parásito, y qué ocurre a continuación.
item.dc:creator: Simar Bajaj and Jackson Gibbs

item.title: La salud mental de Trump entra a debate por sus comentarios sobre Irán y el papa
item.description: El comportamiento imprevisible y los comentarios extremos del presidente de EE. UU. en los últimos días han acelerado la discusión sobre si solo es errático o si tiene temas de salud más serios.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Donald Trump en Charlottesville, Virginia, la semana pasada

item.title: Is It Too Much to Ask to Care About Sudan?
item.description: I have seen what can happen when Americans care about a wrong in the world.
item.dc:creator: Ann Curry
item.category: United Nations High Commission for Refugees

item.title: New Rules Hinder Foreign Firms From Moving Supply Chains From China
item.description: Multinationals in China are concerned that the regulations could allow authorities to penalize companies and executives for shifting supply chains away from the country.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher

media:content.media:description: Steel pipes await export in Lianyungang, China. The country’s trade surplus reached nearly $1.2 trillion last year as exports continued to outpace imports.

item.title: Trump publicó una foto suya como Jesús, la borró y ahora busca justificarse
item.description: La imagen mostraba al presidente Trump bañado en luz divina y vestido con túnicas religiosas. Su interpretación fue que la imagen lo representaba como un médico, no como Jesucristo.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump dijo el lunes que creía que la imagen que había publicado en su cuenta de Truth Social no le representaba como Jesús, sino como médico.

item.title: Netflix Leader Makes Rare Overture to Cinema Owners
item.description: Ted Sarandos attended a major movie theater conference for the first time and met with domestic and international owners, people familiar with the meetings said.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Sperling

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ted Sarandos, Netflix’s co-chief executive, in January. He held two separate meetings on Sunday with top domestic and international theater owners, four people said.

item.title: With Algeria Visit, Pope Leo Returns to His Augustinian Roots
item.description: Some theologians say the pontiff’s understanding of Augustine’s teachings helped inform his response to President Trump’s negative comments about him.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo in Algiers, Algeria, on Monday.

item.title: Corrections: April 14, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, April 14, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Viktor Orban, Populist, Lost His Popularity
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, April 14, 2026.

item.title: Greg Abbott Threatens to Cut $110 Million From Houston Police Over ICE Policy Dispute
item.description: The office of Gov. Greg Abbott wants the city to stop enforcing a new ordinance governing how its officers engage with ICE.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman

media:content.media:description: One of the sponsors of the ordinance accused Gov. Greg Abbott, above, of “attempting to defund the police.”

item.title: Storms Leave a Trail of Warnings Across the Midwest
item.description: A tornado hit a city in Kansas, and forecasters warned of severe weather from Texas to New England as storms move through this week.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar and Erin McCann

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 14, 2026
item.description: High-fives all around for Mark Diehl’s puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Peter Blair

media:content.media:description: Got a nose for puzzles?

item.title: Mapa: un terremoto de magnitud 5,7 sacude Nevada
item.description: Mira la localización del epicentro del sismo y el área de movimiento.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee, Bea Malsky and Víctor Hernández Marroquín

item.title: Map: 5.7-Magnitude Earthquake Shakes Nevada
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee and Bea Malsky

item.title: Man Held in Attack on OpenAI Chief’s Home Had List of A.I. Leaders, Officials Say
item.description: The authorities said a 20-year-old Texas man charged with throwing a homemade bomb at the gate of Sam Altman’s home had written at length about the threat of A.I.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Natallie Rocha

media:content.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Sam Altman, OpenAI’s chief executive, wrote after the attack that the debate over artificial intelligence had become too heated.

item.title: U.S. Is Negotiating an Iran Deal That Would Buy Time, Again
item.description: The United States proposed a 20-year “suspension” of all nuclear activity, even as President Trump demands assurances that Iran can never build a nuclear weapon.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance arriving at Joint Base Andrews from Islamabad, Pakistan, on Sunday. Mr. Vance there were “some good conversations” with Iran in Pakistan, and the ball is now in Tehran’s court.

item.title: U.S. Military Strikes Another Boat in Pacific, Killing 2
item.description: The attack was the 49th since the campaign against vessels in the Caribbean and the eastern Pacific started in early September.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Southern Command, led by Gen. Francis L. Donovan of the Marine Corps, announced the strike on Monday.

item.title: Oasis, Phil Collins and Sade to Join Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: Billy Idol, Luther Vandross and Wu-Tang Clan will also be inducted, while New Edition, Mariah Carey and Melissa Etheridge failed to make the final cut.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor
item.category: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame and Museum

item.title: Michigan Man Is Released in Wife’s Disappearance in the Bahamas
item.description: Brian Hooker had been in police custody since April 8 after he said his wife fell off their boat and was swept away by strong currents.
item.dc:creator: Christine Hauser

item.title: Trump and MAGA Embraced Orban. Now, They’re Worried by His Loss in Hungary’s Election.
item.description: Viktor Orban, the Hungarian prime minister, and his right-wing playbook were embraced by parts of the American right. Now some are worried by his defeat.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning and Shane Goldmacher

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Many of President Trump’s supporters have seen Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary as a kindred spirit, and as an incubator of ideas that they embraced.

item.title: Sign Up for the Book Review’s 2026 Challenge
item.description: (It’s about poetry. And you’ll love it.)
item.dc:creator: The New York Times Books Staff

item.title: Vance Says Pope Leo Should Stay Out of U.S. Affairs
item.description: The vice president, a Catholic, defended President Trump, who had accused the pope of being too liberal and “weak on crime.” The pontiff has said that he has “no fear of the Trump administration.”
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron

media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance in Pakistan on Sunday.

item.title: Youths Who Sued Trump Over Orders to ‘Unleash’ Energy Try to Revive Case
item.description: Appealing the dismissal of their suit, they argued that executive orders to promote fossil fuels endangered their futures and violated their constitutional rights.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zraick

media:content.media:credit: Amanda Lucier for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Julia Olson, a lawyer for the plaintiffs, arriving at court on Monday.

item.title: About 90 People Detained at N.Y.C. Protest Over Arms Sales to Israel
item.description: The protesters sat in the middle of Third Avenue in Midtown Manhattan and blocked traffic for about an hour.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman, Olivia Bensimon and Wesley Parnell
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The New Aesthetic of ‘Euphoria’
item.description: “Euphoria,” the HBO Max show depicting Gen Z, has released its final season. Three of our Style reporters — Gina Cherelus, Jacob Gallagher and Callie Holtermann — discuss the show’s new western aesthetic.
item.dc:creator: Gina Cherelus, Jacob Gallagher, Callie Holtermann, Léo Hamelin and Gabriel Blanco

item.title: Why Are New York Restaurants Starting to Look Like Offices?
item.description: With more people resuming their commutes post-pandemic, the fantasy of a well-designed, well-run workplace beckons.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Sugar

item.title: Amid War, G.O.P. Puts Off Testimony From Senior Pentagon Commanders
item.description: House lawmakers had been scheduled to hear testimony next week from the head of military operations in the Middle East, but the Republican majority postponed the hearing until late May.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “We are six weeks into this conflict, and we still haven’t gotten a public briefing from anyone in the administration about the war,” said Representative Adam Smith of Washington, the ranking Democrat on the Armed Services Committee.

item.title: Tony Gonzales, a Texas Congressman, Says He Will Resign
item.description: The third-term Republican lawmaker is accused of coercing a staff member into a sexual relationship.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Tony Gonzales during a House Committee on Homeland Security hearing in Washington in February.

item.title: What Happens in California Governor’s Race After Eric Swalwell Stepped Down?
item.description: For Democrats, the drama is reverberating well beyond the state.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Eric Swalwell of California

item.title: 11 Best Coachella 2026 Looks: Justin Justin Bieber, Addison Rae and More
item.description: The fashion at the festival is rarely rooted in reality. That’s what makes it fun.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Rotunno
item.category: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival

media:content.media:description: FKA Twigs’s performance attire included a chain-mail hood that she removed to reveal tightly braided red hair.

item.title: The U.S. Blockades Iran’s Ports
item.description: Also, Trump clashes with the pope. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz, off the coast of Oman, on Sunday.

item.title: South Korea Can’t Build Enough Stadiums to Host Its K-Pop Stars
item.description: South Korea doesn’t have enough arenas to show off its own A-list talent. That’s why BTS began its world tour in a once-neglected, 22-year-old soccer stadium.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon

media:content.media:description: Fans gathering outside Goyang Stadium ahead of BTS’s world tour opener in Goyang, South Korea, last week.

item.title: Lena Dunham Is Still Trying to Figure Out Why People Hated Her So Much
item.description: Lena Dunham sits down with The Interview co-host, David Marchese, to discuss her new memoir “Famesick.” Editors’ Note: This conversation omitted the outcome of rape allegations against the “Girls” writer Murray Miller. The Los Angeles District Attorney’s Office did not press charges against Mr. Miller, who denied the accusation.

item.title: What the U.S. Blockade of Iran and the Strait of Hormuz Might Look Like
item.description: The U.S. military has provided few details on how it might carry out President Trump’s orders as he seeks to pressure Tehran on a peace deal. But history and established practices offer some clues.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A ship in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman on Sunday.

item.title: Eric Swalwell Says He Will Resign From Congress After Sex Abuse Accusations
item.description: The California lawmaker said in a social media post Monday that he would quit. He has denied accounts of abusing women.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold, Jill Cowan and Emily Cochrane

media:content.media:description: Representative Eric Swalwell of California had been in a crowded field vying to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom.
